description: Before I knew what cybersecurity was, I was taking things apart to see how they broke. That instinct — find the weak point, understand the system — is what pulled me away from civil and systems engineering and straight into security.

I studied Software Systems Engineering at the University of Regina. Alongside coursework, I worked safety compliance at a refinery — where a missed check could cost lives. That environment taught me something no classroom does: attention to detail isn't optional when the stakes are high. That mindset transferred directly to security.

I'm a Network Security Engineer Intern at ProSec India, deploying firewalls, VPNs, IDS/IPS, and SIEM solutions on live enterprise infrastructure. Outside work, I build home labs — Wazuh SIEM, pfSense firewalls, Azure security stacks — and compete on Hack The Box and TryHackMe to stay sharp on the offensive side. I'm pursuing the AZ-500 certification and looking for roles in security engineering, SOC analysis, or cloud security.
